a:r.a:t: Sales Manager | External Compensation Plan Assessment
HGV uses a more complex, multi-metric structure
(NSV splits, DC contribution), while market plans are more streamlined and primarily focused on team sales volume.
HGV has a slightly lower threshold level of performance
(75% threshold), while market plans delay payout until ~90% attainment. However, HGV has 2 levels of acceleration, which aligns with market payout structures.
Market plans incorporate more earnings smoothing (true-ups),
while HGV relies more on fixed monthly and quarterly components tied to specific metrics.
Market places greater emphasis on SPIFFs
and other non-monetary rewards to enhance retention.
HGV Plan (represents Senior Managers at HGV)
Total Compensation Range (i.e., Leverage / Actual potential earnings)
Total NSV (60% sales manager, 20% action line/in-house)
New Owner NSV (60% action line, 20% sales manager, 0% in-house)
in some cases, customer satisfaction and broader business results are used as Quarterly / Annual bonus measures
Accelerators at typically 115% and 130% of performance (levels of payout not specified)
Quarterly / Annual true-ups or bonuses tied to overall results
Mangers often rollout SPIFFs and provide input for STI creation, although they require approval by directors/VPs
STIs: Run for longer durations (up to ~3 months) to encourage behavior not built into the plan
Directors/VPs control SPIF/STI budgets but approval is required prior to launch
Deal-based incentives (e.g., higher payout for larger transactions)
Non-monetary rewards (gift cards, prizes, time-off, experiences)
*Represents actual earnings data from 1/1/2025 through 12/31/2025, however does not represent mid-year job changes, and therefore might not be accurate for every employee
Where HGV Varies from the Market
